prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Neighbor Who Babysat Too Much
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Divorce Gothic
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Trust isn't given. It's rented.
Omen: a dog barking at an empty gazebo
Relationship dynamic: suspiciously healthy handyman friendship
Props: ceramic owl, scarf with impossible timing, snow globe

Cast:
- Tess Sutton | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=architect | threat_aura=0.32 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=buried one traumatic detail under work for years; has an unsent email that would destroy a family
- Cole Pike | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=marina manager | threat_aura=0.31 | space=annex room described as retreat supplies | secrets=covered for someone out of shame; ignored a warning text on the worst possible night
- Claire Marlowe | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=bartender who knows everybody's business | threat_aura=0.58 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=may die first or survive unbelievably
- Jenna Marlowe | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.79 | space=church basement described as just storage | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Evelyn Sutton | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=hardware store owner | threat_aura=0.27 | space=toolshed described as just storage | secrets=covered for someone out of shame
- Brooke Sutton | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=local podcaster | threat_aura=0.42 | space=marina tackle room described as old family things | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Gavin Lark | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Cole Hale | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Cole Pike is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Tess Sutton says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Cole?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Gavin. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Tess. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The ceramic owl is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Jenna."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
